title: EoE Review
wikitext:
[]: EoE Review             Quote Post by schismatics » Thu Jul 30, 2009 3:42 pm Not sure of where to put this so I planted it here. A pretty good EoE Review

[]:             Quote Post by NickFury90 » Thu Jul 30, 2009 3:45 pm Sssorry, that lisssp wassss ssso irritating. African-American brother sounded like he was trying to do his best Cobra Commander impression.

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Thu Jul 30, 2009 4:01 pm Do not sully the name of the Commander!

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Thu Jul 30, 2009 4:06 pm NickFury90 , I'm not sure how colourful references to Africans apply. ^^;

[]:             Quote Post by NickFury90 » Thu Jul 30, 2009 4:18 pm Its a habit. And I'm black, not "African", "African American" or any other PC term they're using these days.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Jul 30, 2009 4:49 pm Nick wasn't using it in a racial sense.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Thu Jul 30, 2009 6:37 pm NickFury90 wrote: Its a habit. And I'm black, not "African", "African American" or any other PC term they're using these days.

[]:             Quote Post by NickFury90 » Thu Jul 30, 2009 7:43 pm thewayneiac wrote: NickFury90 wrote: Its a habit. And I'm black, not "African", "African American" or any other PC term they're using these days.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Thu Jul 30, 2009 9:32 pm I like the review. However the guy has a lisp which makes it sound kinda funny.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Thu Jul 30, 2009 9:50 pm NickFury90 wrote: I also like how he's apparently an African AMERICAN, despite being in Africa. How the hell would know, dude could be from Britain or something. LOL.

[]:             Quote Post by Fredrick2003 » Thu Jul 30, 2009 10:29 pm Eh... Well he certainly doesn't have a career in radio...

[]:             Quote Post by roblucci01 » Fri Jul 31, 2009 9:28 am OtakucraveTV.... I know that dude. He was his own website with other anime reviews/ podcasts, a lot of them being Eva- related. His stuff is pretty good. I think he's on this forum too.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jul 31, 2009 11:16 am Where the hell is this thread going? And wasn't this supposed to be about EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Fri Jul 31, 2009 11:27 am I get equally flustered when my fellow non-heterosexuals refer to each other lovingly as "faggot" or "carpet-muncher". Sorry, I'm easily distracted by group-based pejoratives. ^^; Anyway, umm, not everyone has a "radio voice", that's for sure. I think there should be a law against letting anyone but dusty English professors narrate things. Richard Attenborough should be appointed by the UN as the official narrator of everything. I do so decree!

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Fri Jul 31, 2009 12:01 pm oOoOoOo wrote: Richard Attenborough should be appointed by the UN as the official narrator of everything. I do so decree!

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Jul 31, 2009 12:17 pm

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Fri Jul 31, 2009 12:22 pm I'm assuming this thread is about a review of End of Evangelion. In which case we're discussing the review. Including the incredible narration. Don't be so confused Ursus-san! I want to see your comprehending face. P.S. I meant David the naturalist, not Richard the actor. Oopsy~

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Fri Jul 31, 2009 12:23 pm I thought we were critiquing the critic? anyway, as far as narrators go, you all are forgetting this guy

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Jul 31, 2009 12:37 pm Damn! Mr. 9000 beat me to it.
